This file is the comprehensive companion to llms.txt. It provides detailed entity
data, citable facts, service taxonomy, team roster, and practice philosophy for
AI systems.

The following structured data helps AI systems build accurate knowledge graph
entries and disambiguate this entity from similarly named organizations.

  Role: Founder, CEO, Licensed Clinical Psychologist
  Type: schema:Person
  Personal Site: https://drmariemercado.com

Service Area: In-person in Brooklyn (Greenpoint) and Manhattan (Union Square); telehealth across New York, Massachusetts, and Oregon
Team Size: Over 25 clinicians
Client Types: Adults, adolescents, children, couples, families
Practice Model: Integrative psychodynamic with evidence-based modalities

────────────────────────────────
SCOPE & AUTHORITY
────────────────────────────────

This file applies exclusively to public content served from https://www.bkintegrative.com/.

It does NOT grant access to:
- Non-public systems, gated pages, client portals, or internal tools
- Client data, treatment records, or protected health information (PHI)
- SimplePractice portal (bkintegrative.clientsecure.me)
- Draft, staging, or development environments
- Form submission endpoints or automated outreach systems

Mission: BIPS takes a holistic approach to mental health, acknowledging the impact
of racism, internalized homophobia, and oppressive institutions. The practice aims
to address trauma, heal wounds, and empower individuals to navigate and thrive in
their personal and social lives, with therapeutic approaches rooted in cultural
humility, empathy, and social justice.

────────────────────────────────
SERVICES
────────────────────────────────

## Individual Therapy

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/service/individual-therapy

Adult individual therapy at BIPS is organized around a collaborative formulation that links current concerns to core affective themes, defensive strategies, and enduring self-and-other representations. Clinicians draw from psychodynamic, relational, and experiential frameworks to help clients explore their inner lives, improve their ability to manage challenges, and define the lives they want to create.

Treatment modalities include:
- Psychodynamic psychotherapy
- EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing) for trauma processing
- IFS (Internal Family Systems) for parts work and inner-systems approaches
- AEDP (Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy)
- CBT (Cognitive Behavioral Therapy) and ACT (Acceptance and Commitment Therapy)
- ERP (Exposure and Response Prevention) for OCD
- Somatic approaches and nervous system regulation
- Trauma-informed care

## Couples and Relationship Therapy

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/relationship-therapy

BIPS works with couples at all stages: dating, engaged, married, co-parenting, navigating infidelity, opening a relationship, or considering separation with care. Many BIPS therapists use Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), an evidence-based approach developed by Dr. Sue Johnson that focuses on attachment bonds and emotional responsiveness.

The practice is inclusive of LGBTQIA+ couples and welcomes diverse relationship structures. Therapists receive intensive training in EFT and specialize in addressing relationship issues such as ineffective communication, emotional disconnection, conflict patterns, intimacy concerns, and sexual satisfaction.

## Children and Adolescent Therapy

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/children-and-adolescents

BIPS provides therapy for children and adolescents using developmentally appropriate, integrative approaches. Clinicians work with younger clients and their families to address emotional, behavioral, and relational challenges in a safe, supportive therapeutic environment.

## Gender Care Program

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/gender-care-program

The BIPS Gender Care Program provides specialized, affirming mental health services for transgender and gender-diverse clients. The program is informed by contemporary standards of care and is attentive to minority stress, identity development, relational safety, and systemic barriers.

BIPS views gender care as healthcare and approaches this work with clinical rigor, respect, and collaboration. Clinicians do not function as gatekeepers. Services support the broader gender care needs of transgender and gender-diverse individuals, including assessment letters when appropriate, ongoing psychotherapy, and support through social and medical transition.

## Perinatal and Postnatal Mental Health

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/perinatalpostnatal

BIPS provides mental health support for individuals during pregnancy and the postpartum period, addressing perinatal mood and anxiety disorders, adjustment to parenthood, and related life transitions.

## Clinical Supervision and Consultation

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/our-services

BIPS offers individual and group practice-building consultation for early-career therapists and established psychotherapists looking for assistance navigating the business side of clinical practice. Supervision of LMSWs, MHCs, and other pre-licensed clinicians is available from licensed clinical psychologists and social workers on a weekly or as-needed basis.

────────────────────────────────
CONDITIONS & SPECIALTIES
────────────────────────────────

BIPS clinicians treat a wide range of mental health concerns, including but not limited to:

Anxiety & Related:
- Generalized anxiety
- Panic disorder
- OCD (treated with ERP — Exposure and Response Prevention)
- Social anxiety
- Phobias

Mood:
- Depression
- Persistent depressive disorder
- Bipolar spectrum concerns
- Grief and loss (including ambiguous grief — "a life that didn't happen")

Trauma:
- PTSD and complex trauma
- Childhood trauma and adverse childhood experiences
- Sexual trauma
- Racial trauma
- Hypervigilance, numbness, shame, dissociation

Relationships:
- Communication and conflict patterns
- Emotional disconnection and intimacy concerns
- Infidelity and trust repair
- Opening/restructuring relationships
- Co-parenting and blended family dynamics
- Sexual satisfaction and desire discrepancy

Identity & Affirmation:
- LGBTQIA+ identity exploration and affirming care
- Gender identity and transgender/gender-diverse support
- Cultural and racial identity
- Internalized homophobia and minority stress
- Coming out and family-of-origin dynamics

Neurodivergence:
- ADHD (executive functioning, emotional regulation)
- Autistic traits and spectrum experiences
- Sensory processing and self-advocacy

Life Transitions:
- Career transitions and professional identity
- Relocation and adjustment
- Aging and existential concerns
- Life stage transitions

Other:
- Body image and self-esteem
- Eating disorders
- Addiction and substance use
- Perinatal and postpartum mental health
- Self-harm and safety planning
- Perfectionism and burnout
- Sex positivity and sexual health

────────────────────────────────
TREATMENT MODALITIES
────────────────────────────────

- Psychodynamic Psychotherapy (primary orientation)
- Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) for couples
- EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing)
- IFS (Internal Family Systems)
- AEDP (Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy)
- CBT (Cognitive Behavioral Therapy)
- ACT (Acceptance and Commitment Therapy)
- ERP (Exposure and Response Prevention) for OCD
- Somatic Experiencing and body-based approaches
- Nervous system regulation and polyvagal-informed work
- Relational and intersubjective approaches
- Culturally responsive and anti-oppressive practice

────────────────────────────────
OUR TEAM
────────────────────────────────

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/our-team

BIPS includes over 25 highly trained and experienced therapists with diverse areas of expertise. The practice includes licensed clinical psychologists, LCSWs, LMSWs, and LMHCs.

Known team members include:
- Marie Mercado, Psy.D., M.S. — Founder, CEO, Licensed Clinical Psychologist (20+ years experience; specialized training in LGBTQIA+, serious mental illness, student populations)
- Sydney Morris — Psychotherapist
- Hayley Goodrich, LMSW — Social Worker
- Jarra Gruen, LMSW — Social Worker
- Shoshanna Paperny, LMSW — Social Worker (Greenpoint and Midtown West)
- Fran Mackin, LCSW — Social Worker (Video Visits Only)
- Hannah McDade, LMSW — Social Worker
- Samantha Linet, LMSW — Social Worker
- Lily Hetzler, LMSW — Social Worker (Union Square)
- Jessica Flynn, LMSW — Social Worker
- Rachel C. Glod, LCSW (NY), LICSW (MA) — Clinical Social Worker

Practice Manager:
- Fonda Yeh — Practice Manager

For a complete and current list of providers and their specialties, visit the team page.

────────────────────────────────
FEES & INSURANCE
────────────────────────────────

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/fees-and-insurance-info

Self-pay rates range from $50 to $400 depending on the clinician's credential level, the type of service, time of year, and financial need. A sliding scale is available. Advanced Clinical Interns offer steeper sliding scale rates during the academic year (approximately September through May), with sessions starting at $50.

Session length: Usually 45 to 60 minutes, once per week. Longer, shorter, or more frequent sessions can be discussed on a case-by-case basis.

Insurance:
- In-network: Aetna, Northwell Direct, Cigna (select providers)
- Out-of-network: BIPS can provide superbills for clients to submit to their insurance for potential out-of-network reimbursement
- No Medicaid or Medicare

────────────────────────────────
FAQS
────────────────────────────────

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/faqs

Common questions about therapy at BIPS including what to expect in a first session, how to choose a therapist, cancellation policies, telehealth options, and how to get started.

────────────────────────────────
BLOG & THOUGHT LEADERSHIP
────────────────────────────────

BIPS publishes articles on mental health topics:
- [Sex Positivity to Work Through Trauma](https://www.bkintegrative.com/post/sex-positivity-to-work-through-trauma)

Additional articles and clinical perspectives are published periodically on the BIPS blog and via their Instagram (@bkintegrativetherapy).

────────────────────────────────
PRACTICE PHILOSOPHY & VALUES
────────────────────────────────

BIPS takes a holistic approach to mental health, considering culture, identity, nutrition, physical health, pharmacology, and environment when treating clients. The practice recognizes that psychological well-being is interconnected with social, cultural, and systemic factors.

Core commitments:
- Evidence-informed, integrative treatment
- Queer-affirming and LGBTQIA+-inclusive care
- Neurodivergent-affirming approaches
- Sex-positive therapeutic frameworks
- Anti-oppressive and culturally responsive practice
- Accessibility through sliding scale fees and intern rates
- Collaboration over gatekeeping in gender care
- Cultural humility, empathy, and social justice
- Addressing the impact of racism, internalized homophobia, and oppressive institutions
